title: astragalus longiracemosus
intro: Astragalus longiracemosus, also known as Long-branched Milk Vetch, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the legume family. It is native to China and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as boosting the immune system, reducing fatigue, and improving cardiovascular health. Astragalus longiracemosus is typically prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
